name: Gemma 4 26B A4B
archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Transformer — 128 total experts (8 active + 1 shared routed per token), 30 layers, 1024-token sliding-window local attention, multimodal text+image encoder (~550M params)
note: The MoE sibling of the dense Gemma 4 31B, flagged as missing in the dense model's own verificationNotes and now confirmed directly: 128 experts, 8 active + 1 shared per token, near-31B quality while running about as fast as a 4B model.

results.notes: 26B A4B instruction-tuned, from the official Gemma 4 benchmark table (same table/page confirms dense 31B's 85.2, which matches the already-verified corpus record for gemma-4.json exactly, corroborating the table's authenticity).
Codeforces Elo, 26B A4B (dense 31B scores 2150 on the same table).
MMMU-Pro (vision), 26B A4B; also independently listed in the model repo's structured eval_results (.eval_results/mmmu_pro.yaml, value 73.8).

Humanity's Last Exam, no tools, 26B A4B. Also independently confirmed via the HF repo's structured eval_results metadata (HLE-no-tools 8.7). Card also lists an HLE-with-search score of 17.2, which is a distinct tool-augmented setting not captured by the single 'hle' taxonomy slug and was omitted to avoid conflating settings.
Multilingual MMLU, 26B A4B, from the official Gemma 4 benchmark table.

26B A4B column of Table 5 ('Performance comparison of Gemma 3 27B and Gemma 4 models on diverse benchmarks') in the Gemma 4 Technical Report. Verified against the raw HTML table cells (arxiv.org/html/2607.02770, table id S2.T5, column order 31B/26B-A4B/12B/E4B/E2B/Gemma3-27B confirmed by cross-checking the 31B and 26B-A4B MMLU-Pro/AIME/GPQA cells against the already-verified values on file for both this model and the dense gemma-4.json sibling).
26B A4B column of Table 5 in the Gemma 4 Technical Report (arxiv 2607.02770). Verified against raw HTML table cells; same table/column-order corroboration as the ifbench entry.

26B A4B column of Table 5, row 'Tau2 - telecom' (Table 5 also reports separate airline=76.0 and retail=85.5 rows, which have no matching taxonomy slug and were omitted). Verified against raw HTML table cells. Also found on the same table: 'Big Bench Extra Hard micro avg' (64.8) and 'Terminal Bench Hard' (14.0) for the 26B A4B column -- both deliberately NOT mapped to the generic 'big-bench'/'terminal-bench' taxonomy slugs since they are explicitly named as distinct, harder benchmark variants (BBEH and a 'Hard' subset of Terminal-Bench), not the same evaluation as the base benchmarks. 'HLE with search' (17.2) also appears on this table but was skipped since 'hle' is already a tracked slug for this model (and that with-search variant was already deliberately excluded per the file's own verificationNotes).

verificationNotes: Closes the gap flagged in gemma-4.json's own verificationNotes ('a separate 26B A4B MoE variant exists'). Confirmed via direct raw-HTML fetch (curl, not the LLM-summarized WebFetch tool) of both https://huggingface.co/google/gemma-4-26B-A4B and .../gemma-4-26B-A4B-it: both return HTTP 200 with real repo markers (Files-and-versions/tree/main/colab/kaggle links, safetensors index totalling ~26.5B params, license apache-2.0, tag arxiv:2607.02770) -- verified this isn't a 404 by diffing against a deliberately fake HF repo slug, which returned 401 with literal '404'/'Sorry' text, unlike the real pages. The -it page's rendered README contains an explicit 'Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Model' properties table (Total Parameters 25.2B, Active Parameters 3.8B, 30 layers, 256K context, 262K vocab, 8 active/128 total experts + 1 shared, Text+Image modalities, ~550M vision encoder) and a 5-column benchmark table (Gemma 4 31B / 26B A4B / E4B / E2B / Gemma 3 27B). Critically, the 31B column in that same table (MMLU-Pro 85.2, AIME 89.2, GPQA-Diamond 84.3) matches the already-verified numbers on file in corpus/models/google/gemma-4.json exactly, which strongly corroborates the 26B A4B column is the same authentic Google-published table. Structured HF eval_results metadata (contributed by HF/Google staffer 'merve' via PR #3) independently lists GPQA-diamond 82.3, MMLU-Pro 82.6, MMMU-Pro-vision 73.8, HLE-no-tools 8.7 -- all consistent with the rendered table. Independently corroborated (second, non-Google, non-HF source) via a direct fetch of https://openrouter.ai/google/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it:free, which independently describes it as 'an instruction-tuned Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model from Google DeepMind', Apache-2.0, 262,144-token context (=256K), hf_slug google/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it, a Cloudflare-Workers-AI provider listing dated 2026-04-03 (one day after the dense 31B's 2026-04-02 launch), and states it 'Includes independent benchmarks from Artificial Analysis.' The Gemma 4 Technical Report (arxiv 2607.02770) was confirmed real via a direct fetch of its Hugging Face papers mirror (title: 'Gemma 4 Technical Report'). ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/core/model_card_4 was fetched directly and confirmed to exist in the live Google AI for Developers site nav (title 'Gemma 4 model card', with a 'Gemma 4 license' entry under Legal), matching the dense entry's docs link and the shared HF README's family blurb ('Featuring both Dense and Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures'); its benchmark content itself is client-side JS-rendered and could not be scraped statically in this sandbox, so HF is used as the results sourceUrl since that content was directly verified byte-for-byte. Could not directly fetch blog.google's Gemma 4 launch post in this sandbox (network truncated repeatedly) but a WebSearch snippet independently paraphrased it as describing four sizes 'Effective 2B (E2B), Effective 4B (E4B), 26B Mixture of Experts (MoE) and 31B Dense', consistent with everything above; treated as corroborating-but-not-primary since it wasn't fetched directly. releaseDate 2026-04-02 assumed to match the dense sibling's family-wide launch date (OpenRouter's Cloudflare provider listing appearing 2026-04-03 is consistent with next-day provider onboarding, not a later model release). No modality/audio/video benchmark claims included since not confirmed on the primary table (OpenRouter's input_modalities listed video in addition to image/text, but the HF property table only states 'Text, Image' -- the video claim was not corroborated on the primary source and is omitted here).
